steps.title: Identify the main subject
steps.content: The subject is Objects, which is plural. The phrase “such as 2010 BL4, a 16-kilometer-wide body first detected in 2010” doesn't change the subject.

steps.title: Apply subject-verb agreement
steps.content:
Identify the grammar issue: subject-verb agreement. Rule: a plural subject takes the base form of the verb (no -s/-es).
  • Choice A — "exhibits": singular present-tense verb (third-person -s); disagrees with plural "Objects." ✗
  • Choice B — "exhibit": base-form (plural) present-tense verb; agrees with plural "Objects." ✓
  • Choice C — "is exhibiting": singular present-progressive verb; disagrees with plural subject. ✗
  • Choice D — "has exhibited": singular present-perfect verb; disagrees with plural subject. ✗
That matches choice B — B is correct.
